source.catalog_ref: I-EU8 (I-LA thread collegiate formula-class racecar benchmark Brasil)

full_prompt: Design an impact attenuator for an collegiate formula-class racecar benchmark Brasil 2025 car (handbook V3 + addendum V1). collegiate formula-class racecar benchmark rules require the impact attenuator to decelerate a 300 kg mass at 7 m/s (49 J/kg, total 7350 J kinetic energy) with peak deceleration no greater than 40 g and average deceleration no greater than 20 g. Minimum envelope: 200 mm length x 200 mm width x 200 mm height (per collegiate formula-class racecar benchmark IA requirement). Must attach to the Anti-Intrusion Plate (AIP) via 4 M8 A2-70 bolts through a 4 mm aluminum mounting plate. Attenuator is an aluminum 6061-T6 honeycomb block (core density 83 kg/m^3, crush strength 2.41 MPa per MatWeb AL 6061-T6 3/16 in cell 0.003 wall, analogous to standard collegiate formula-class racecar benchmark AIA datasheet value), surrounded by a 1.0 mm 6061-T6 aluminum skin formed as an open-top box. Full honeycomb cell axis aligned along crush direction. Load cases (collegiate formula-class racecar benchmark IA is a crash case): (LC1) nominal crash: 300 kg mass at 7 m/s impacts attenuator axially; verify 7350 J absorbed over <=150 mm crush stroke with peak <=40 g and average <=20 g (explicit dynamics or simplified 1D energy-stroke analysis); (LC2) oblique crash: 15 degree off-axis impact at 7 m/s on 300 kg, still must not punch through AIP; (LC3) post-crash residual: after crush, the attenuator must remain attached to the AIP by the 4 M8 bolts - compute bolt shear after typical post-crash offset force of 5 kN; (LC4) handling / towing: 500 N horizontal static load at attenuator front face (non-crash). Pass/fail: (R1) energy absorption under LC1 must be >= 7350 J over <=150 mm stroke (R1 is the primary collegiate formula-class racecar benchmark gate); (R2) peak deceleration under LC1 must be <= 40 g; (R3) average deceleration under LC1 must be <= 20 g; (R4) under LC2 oblique, AIP must not yield (max local stress <= yield 276 MPa); (R5) attenuator dry mass (core + skin + mount plate) must not exceed 3.5 kg; (R6) M8 bolt shear stress under LC3 must remain below 180 MPa shear (approx 0.5 x 360 MPa tensile yield for A2-70). Thermal, fluid, radiation, and vibration analyses not required. Buckling of the outer skin is captured via the crush-stroke calculation. Deliverables: STEP AP242 of honeycomb core + skin + mount plate as assembly, material datasheet reference, crush-stroke vs force curve computed via 1D analytical + verified by explicit dynamics, acceleration trace, bolt shear calculation, mass budget.
short_prompt:
Design an impact attenuator for a collegiate formula-class racecar benchmark Brasil 2025 car (handbook V3 + addendum V1). Rules: decelerate a 300 kg mass at 7 m/s (49 J/kg, 7350 J KE), peak <= 40 g, average <= 20 g. Minimum envelope 200 x 200 x 200 mm. Attach to the AIP via 4 x M8 A2-70 through a 4 mm aluminum mounting plate.

Envelope: 6061-T6 aluminum honeycomb block (core 83 kg/m^3, crush 2.41 MPa, cells aligned with the crush axis); 1.0 mm 6061-T6 open-top-box skin around the core.

Factor regime: IA is a crash case - explicit-dynamics gates on energy and decel; AIP local yield (276 MPa); A2-70 M8 shear allowable 180 MPa; IA dry mass (core + skin + mount plate) <= 3.5 kg.

Load cases.
- Nominal crash: 300 kg at 7 m/s axial; verify 7350 J over <= 150 mm stroke, peak <= 40 g, avg <= 20 g.
- Oblique crash: 15 deg off-axis at 7 m/s on 300 kg (no AIP punch-through).
- Post-crash residual: 4 M8 bolts holding the attenuator to the AIP after a typical 5 kN offset force.
- Handling / towing: 500 N horizontal static on the attenuator front face.

requires_non_fea_solver.reason: collegiate formula-class racecar benchmark impact-attenuator 7350 J crash is a nonlinear crush event; primary verification is explicit dynamics (LS-DYNA / Abaqus Explicit / Radioss), not linear FEA.
collegiate formula-class racecar benchmark IA Data Report allows verification via 1D energy + crush stroke + datasheet honeycomb crush curve, which is accepted in lieu of full dynamic simulation.
examples: Excel/Python 1D calc per collegiate formula-class racecar benchmark IA Data Report template

prompt.title: collegiate formula-class racecar benchmark Brasil 2025 Impact Attenuator
prompt.brief: Aluminum 6061-T6 honeycomb IA, 200x200x200 mm min, absorb 7350 J on 300 kg @ <=40 g peak <=20 g avg.

recommended_fea_setup.element_type: explicit shell/solid for skin; crushable foam material model for core

exclusions_explained.vibration_analysis: One-shot crash event, no modal gate.
exclusions_explained.buckling_analysis: Skin crush and core crush modeled in nonlinear crush curve, not linear eigenvalue.

eval_coverage: collegiate formula-class racecar benchmark IA energy gate
